subject: skills/leash_for_hooks/lib/audit.py — audit budget metric
principal: The audit budget is enforced as 'substantive lines of code <= N' (80 for collectors, 60 for resolvers, 150 for orchestration). Line count is a poor proxy for what the audit is supposed to detect — quiet rewrites that change behavior. A collector under 80 lines but with deeply nested branching can hide as much intent as a 200-line straight-line function.
interest: A reviewer skimming a diff cannot tell whether complexity grew, only whether length did. The audit produces false confidence — passes that don't mean what they claim to mean.
payoff: Replace the LOC budget with cyclomatic complexity (McCabe 1976; NIST SP 500-235; Pylint/Ruff/Radon defaults of <= 10 per function). Use radon (https://radon.readthedocs.io/) to compute per-function complexity in audit_program(). Audit budget rule becomes 'every function <= 10 cyclomatic complexity, module <= 30 cumulative.'
re_trigger: a collector under 80 LOC ships a behavior change that should have been caught by the audit but wasn't (i.e., the false-confidence this debt warns about manifests)

closure.evidence: 40f8274 — audit.py rewritten to compute McCabe via radon.cc_visit; budgets shifted from LOC (80/60/150) to cumulative cyclomatic (≤30/module, ≤10/function); new violation codes function_complexity_exceeded and module_complexity_exceeded replace audit_budget_exceeded. New audit surfaces 2 pre-existing violators (hook_config:_walk_settings, data_point_resolver:resolve) — tracked as D-007.
